bill.short_title: Further Additional Continuing Appropriations and Other Extensions Act, 2024

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 106(3)

provisions.description: Extends the continuing resolution end date specified in section 106(3) of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024, to March 8, 2024.
notes: SEC. 101(1) of this bill amends P.L. 118-15 Division A section 106(3)
This changes the expiration date for the first tranche of CR-funded accounts
provisions.raw_text: by striking the date specified in section 106(3) and inserting ''March 8, 2024''

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 106(4)

provisions.description: Changes the date specified in section 106(4) of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024, to March 1, 2024.
notes: SEC. 101(2) of this bill amends P.L. 118-15 Division A section 106(4)
This changes the expiration date for the second tranche of CR-funded accounts (the 'laddered' CR structure)
provisions.raw_text: by striking the date specified in section 106(4) and inserting ''March 1, 2024''

cross_references.description: Extends duration from 94 days to 129 days
cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 123

provisions.description: Amends section 123 of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024, to change the duration from 94 days to 129 days and from 94-day to 129-day.
notes: SEC. 101(3) of this bill amends P.L. 118-15 Division A section 123
Extends a duration parameter from 94 days to 129 days, consistent with the extended CR period
provisions.raw_text: in section 123, by striking ''94 days'' and inserting ''129 days'' and by striking ''94-day'' and inserting ''129-day''

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 124

notes: SEC. 101(4) of this bill amends section 124 of P.L. 118-15 Division A
Increases the dollar amount in section 124 from $663,070,000 to $2,199,260,000, a net increase of $1,536,190,000
The specific account governed by section 124 is not named in this amendment text; it is defined in the original section 124 of P.L. 118-15

provisions.account_name: Department of Energy—Atomic Energy Defense Activities—National Nuclear Security Administration—Weapons Activities

cross_references.description: Amounts made available under the CR baseline
cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 101

provisions.description: Allows amounts for NNSA Weapons Activities to be apportioned up to the rate necessary to mitigate issuing WARN notices for the Uranium Processing Facility at Y-12, not to exceed $760,000,000.
notes: Added to P.L. 118-15 Division A as new section 147 by SEC. 101(5) of this bill
This is a CR anomaly allowing higher apportionment rate for a specific construction project
The $760,000,000 is a ceiling ('not to exceed'), not new budget authority
Purpose is to prevent issuance of WARN Act layoff notices for the Uranium Processing Facility project at Y-12 National Security Complex
provisions.raw_text: Amounts made available by section 101 for ''Department of Energy—Atomic Energy Defense Activities—National Nuclear Security Administration—Weapons Acti

deadlines: 3 days after each use of SEC. 147 authority
provisions.description: Requires the OMB Director to notify the Appropriations Committees within 3 days of each use of SEC. 147 authority, and requires the Secretary of Energy to provide weekly reports specifying obligations incurred for the Uranium Processing Facility purposes.
notes: SEC. 147(b), added to P.L. 118-15 Division A by SEC. 101(5) of this bill
Two separate reporting requirements: OMB notification within 3 days of each use, and DOE weekly obligation reports
provisions.raw_text: The Director of the Office of Management and Budget shall notify the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate not la

cross_references.description: Amounts made available under the CR baseline
cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 101

provisions.description: Allows amounts for FAA Operations to be apportioned up to the rate necessary to fund mandatory pay increases, inflationary adjustments, maintain and improve air traffic services, hire and train air traffic controllers, and continue aviation safety oversight, while avoiding service reductions.
notes: Added to P.L. 118-15 Division A as new section 148 by SEC. 101(5) of this bill
This is a CR anomaly allowing higher apportionment rate for FAA Operations without a specific dollar ceiling
Permits spending above the standard CR rate for enumerated purposes: pay raises, inflation, air traffic services, controller hiring/training, safety oversight
Classified as 'other' rather than 'limitation' because no specific dollar ceiling is stated
provisions.raw_text: Amounts made available by section 101 for ''Department of Transportation—Federal Aviation Administration—Operations'' may be apportioned up to the rate

provisions.description: Exempts the budgetary effects of Division B from statutory PAYGO scorecards, Senate PAYGO scorecards, and budget scorekeeping under the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act and the Congressional Budget Act.
notes: Subsections (a)-(c). Standard PAYGO/budget scorekeeping exemption for the division. Exempts from statutory PAYGO, Senate PAYGO, and CBO/OMB baseline scorekeeping.
provisions.raw_text: The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go A
provisions.title: IV

flagged_issues: SEC. 101(4) amends section 124 of P.L. 118-15 by increasing dollar amount from $663,070,000 to $2,199,260,000; the specific account governed by that section is not identifiable from this bill's text alone
SEC. 148 allows higher apportionment rate for FAA Operations without specifying a dollar ceiling, making it an open-ended CR anomaly
All substantive provisions in Division A are amendments to the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 (P.L. 118-15 Division A), not freestanding appropriations
SEC. 122 reduces Medicaid Improvement Fund by $655,689,081 (from $5,796,117,810 to $5,140,428,729) as a budget offset
SEC. 132 reduces Medicare Improvement Fund by $53,000,000 (from $2,250,795,056 to $2,197,795,056) as a budget offset
SEC. 141 appropriates 'such sums as may be necessary' with no specific dollar amount
SEC. 142 and SEC. 143 appropriate pro rata portions of FY2023 amounts with no specific dollar figures
No provisions with semantics 'new_budget_authority' — all spending provisions are mandatory_spending_extension type; total new mandatory spending for SEC. 101-102 is approximately $635,858,903
